name: Trombone Shorty
abstract: Troy Andrews (born January 2, 1986), also known by the stage name Trombone Shorty, is an American musician, most notably a trombone player, from New Orleans, Louisiana. His music fuses rock, pop, jazz, funk, and hip hop.

concerts.label: Preservation Hall Jazz Band and Friends
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Germaine Bazzle
about_quotes.evidence: I remember I used to sing with the St. Louis Cathedral Choir... I went to the corner and it’s all these little boys... Trombone Shorty with the trombone... I got so excited I ran inside to the rectory and I said, “You guys got to come out here and see this.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Germaine Bazzle interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 21, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris
about_quotes.evidence: now Nicholas Payton or Trombone Shorty, guys like that that come up from New Orleans, they were all inspired by Louis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 21, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: Trombone Shorty's gonna be there... I'm using a lot of young musicians playing very, very contemporary music.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Wendy Oxenhorn
about_quotes.evidence: I know Trombone Shorty and James Andrews got a horn the next day. They FedExed them to me in Lafayette.
about_quotes.doc_title: Wendy Oxenhorn — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
